topStory.text: Micron raised its planned US investment to more than $250B through 2035 — targeting 40% of its DRAM production in the US — and poured first concrete at its Clay, NY megafab more than a quarter ahead of schedule, alongside up to $3B to strengthen the US supply chain, including $500M in strategic financing for GlobalWafers' Sherman, Texas 300mm wafer fab.

feed.text: SK hynix filed its final F-1 amendment for a ~$29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing (ticker SKHY) — the largest US listing by a foreign company on record, ahead of Alibaba's 2014 debut — with trading set to begin July 10; the filing cites a 56.4% global HBM revenue share (Q1 2026, IDC).

Samsung's preliminary Q2 guidance: a record ~KRW 89.4T (~$58B) operating profit, up ~1,810% year over year on AI memory demand — yet shares closed ~7% lower on concerns the memory upcycle is maturing.

Taiwan's economics ministry approved TSMC's $20B capital injection into TSMC Arizona — the sixth tranche, lifting total cleared US investment to $44B — funding a 12-inch wafer fab and an advanced packaging plant.

NVIDIA unveiled a revenue-sharing financing model for AI clouds — earning standard product revenue plus a share of cloud revenue on the capacity it supports — with first partners Sharon AI (up to 40,000 GB300 GPUs) and Firmus (up to 170,000 GPUs).
Data-center backlash hit Missouri's GOP state-senate primaries, with candidates demanding restrictions in a state that just landed ~$25B of Amazon ($10B, New Florence) and Google ($15B) campuses.

Virginia's first-in-the-nation data-center electricity tax took effect July 1 — $0.011 per kWh on all power consumed by data centers, including self-generated and behind-the-meter supply — expected to raise about $600M a year and adding roughly 10% to a facility's effective power rate (nearly $100M annually for a 1 GW campus).

FERC issued show-cause orders to all six US grid operators, giving each 60 days to justify or rewrite their tariffs for connecting data centers and other gigawatt-scale loads — covering co-location, behind-the-meter generation, flexible-load service and cost-shift protections — plus 30 days to report how they will ensure adequate generation for large loads.

Meta unveiled its first data center in Canada — a more than CA$13B, gigawatt-scale AI campus on 1,750 acres in Sturgeon County, Alberta, to be powered by a new natural-gas plant from a Pembina Pipeline-led consortium — underscoring how US hyperscalers are reaching across the border for land and power amid domestic grid constraints.

Texas's ERCOT 'Batch Zero' process — approved by regulators to study large loads of 75 MW or more as a single cohort — moves through its first developer deadlines this month, as ERCOT reports 438 GW of large-load interconnection requests, nearly 89% from data centers.

Anthropic signed a 20-year, ~$19B lease with TeraWulf for approximately 401 MW of AI compute at the Justified Data campus in Hawesville, Kentucky — among the largest single-tenant AI data-center leases disclosed to date, as AI labs race to lock up long-duration capacity ahead of first operations in late 2027.

PJM's heat-wave demand hit roughly 163 GW on July 2 — just short of the 2006 all-time record of 165,563 MW, and suppressed by demand response — after a 161,910 MW preliminary peak on July 1, with DOE emergency data-center curtailment orders in effect through July 3.

National Grid Ventures agreed to invest $1.75B for a 35% stake in Joulent — whose foundational Project Kilby, a 2.67 GW co-located West Texas power facility built 50/50 with Chevron, will supply a Microsoft-operated data center under a 20-year PPA, with first power targeted by 2028.
